titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Brown to Robert Chalmers, 18 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Robert Brown, the assistant manager at the National Margarine Company Ltd., to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938).In the letter Brown requests compensation for a attending the trail of Henry McCambley (b.1871). Brown was required to attend the hearing as a witness in Belfast. The letter discusses the money Brown lost stating that despite what he is being told, he has yet to receive the full compensation. Brown also discusses how the Belfast Police Authorities misled him regarding the payment. The letter also requests that these facts be given to the chief secretary when he is appointed, due to the vacancy of the position following resignation of Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), due to his political failure during the Easter Rising. Chalmers became under-secretary for a brief period following the resignation of Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), who relinquished his position following his political failure during the 1916 Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5613/8607/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
THE NATIONAL MARGARINE COMPANY LIMITED 10 QUALITY STREET LEITH 18th. May 1916 To The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle, DUBLIN. Dear Sir I am in receipt of your favour of 17th. inst. 8183.
I beg to remind you that per your letter of 18th. April you stated
that I had been paid for expenses at the maximum rate authorized
by the Lords Commissioners of His Majesty's Treasury. I pointed o
out to you in my letter of 22nd. Apl. that I had not received,
as regards the journey of 8th. Oct. the expenses authorised by the
Lords Commissioners to which you referred. You now give a differ
-ent reason altogether, for refusing to make good the loss sustain
-ed by me. There seems to be no consistency between your letter of
18th. April and 17th. inst. In any case it matters little to me what the practice of the
Royal Irish Constabulary consists of. I was requested by the
Belfast Police Authorities who communicated to through the Leith Police
Officials, to proceed to Belfast to give information and I was
informed that my expenses would be allowed by the Belfast Police
authorities. The police authorities had no right to mislead me if
it was not the intention of the parties af responsible to renumer
-ate me loss of earnings. I would not have gone to Belfast under
the conditions stated in your letter of 17th. May. Acting in good
faith I proceeded on a journey which was both inconvenient to me
and full of discomfort. It would appear that I have been trick
-ed by the Police. i wish these remarks to be laid before the
Chief Secretary as soon as he is appointed. I protest against
such usage : and it seems to me that my claim has not received the
consideration which it merits. Once again I request you to order payment of Â£1/1/0 for
loss of time. Yours truly, Rob Brown
